title: stereospermum ghorta
intro: Stereospermum ghorta, also known as the 'Tiger's Eye', is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its attractive yellow and red flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Stereospermum ghorta include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal remedy by decocting its leaves and roots, or by using its flowers in the form of infusions or ointments, to harness its medicinal properties.
